refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple requirements or specify implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety compatibility criteria, such as p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a dedicated low-latency communication protocol optimized for teleoperation
Use edge computing nodes near the worksite to minimize network latency
Design a predictive control algorithm to compensate for communication delays
Segment the teleoperation system into modular components with asynchronous communication to isolate latency effects
Establish a real-time monitoring and adaptive control mechanism to dynamically adjust system parameters based on latency measurements
selected_dp.selected_dp: Use edge computing nodes near the worksite to minimize network latency

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication and control modules
Shared state dependencies causing latency propagation
coupling_check.notes: Selecting edge computing nodes as the DP supports decoupling communication latency from central processing, reducing coupling risks. Other candidate DPs involving predictive control or protocol design may introduce tighter coupling between control and communication functions.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Edge computing resources are available and reliable at the worksite
Operational policies allow deployment of edge nodes
Latency measurements are accurate and representative
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the DP to include modular separation of communication and control functions to further reduce coupling and improve maintainability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module includes the edge computing nodes and associated infrastructure deployed near the worksite to minimize network latency for teleoperation and safety services.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends real-time data from edge nodes to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and tasks to be processed at the edge.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges safety-related alerts with Safety Services module.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles network communication between edge nodes and remote systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as edge computing nodes are physically and functionally part of NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as most processing is localized here.
Vehicle may use edge nodes but does not contain them; high diffusion as edge computing is external to vehicle.
Teleoperation depends on edge nodes for latency reduction but does not own them; moderate diffusion.
Safety Services interact with edge nodes but do not contain them; moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of using edge computing nodes near the worksite, as it physically and functionally encompasses the edge infrastructure, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules in processing tasks at the edge may cause interface complexity.
If edge computing nodes are distributed or virtualized beyond NGHE Solution scope, containment may weaken.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment proves weak due to distributed edge nodes, revise DP to specify edge computing nodes as part of NGHE Solution infrastructure explicitly, clarifying ownership and reducing diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of using edge computing nodes near the worksite directly addresses the FR of supporting teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation by minimizing network latency, which is critical to meeting the latency acceptance criterion (p95 ≤ 150 ms).

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules in processing tasks at the edge may cause interface complexity and diffusion risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is highly cohesive and appropriately contains the DP, as it includes the edge computing nodes and associated infrastructure. Containment scores are high (9) with low diffusion (2), indicating good packaging. However, moderate diffusion scores in related modules (Teleoperation, Safety Services) suggest some risk of diffusion if processing responsibilities are not clearly partitioned.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end latency in the NGHE Solution module under agreed network conditions and ensuring compliance with the safety plan and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The IM boundary clearly includes the edge computing nodes responsible for minimizing latency, aligning well with the DP and FR. Interfaces are well defined for telemetry, tasking, safety alerts, and network links, supporting clear allocation of responsibilities.

recommended_revisions: If containment proves weak due to distributed or virtualized edge nodes beyond the NGHE Solution scope, revise the DP to explicitly specify that edge computing nodes are part of the NGHE Solution infrastructure to clarify ownership and reduce diffusion risk.
Clarify and document the processing responsibilities at the edge between NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to minimize interface complexity and diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP and IM are well aligned with the FR, and containment and diffusion risks are low to moderate and manageable through documentation and clarification rather than further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clear module boundaries and verification plans is sufficient at this level without further sub-FR derivation.

revise.why: If containment weakens due to distributed or virtualized edge nodes beyond NGHE Solution scope, revise DP to explicitly specify edge computing nodes as part of NGHE Solution infrastructure to clarify ownership and reduce diffusion risk.

assumptions: Edge computing nodes are physically and logically contained within NGHE Solution module.
Interfaces between NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules are well defined and manageable.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity and diffusion risk if processing responsibilities at the edge are not clearly partitioned among modules.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing end-to-end latency meets p95 ≤ 150 ms under operational conditions.
Documentation clarifying processing responsibilities and ownership boundaries among NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
